symptoms.symptom_name: New unilateral (right) temporal headache in an older adult

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: New lateralised temporal headache after age 50 is the sentinel symptom of GCA and mandates exclusion.
Compatible with tension headache but non-specific; cannot distinguish benign from arteritic on its own.

symptoms.symptom_name: One week of constitutional fatigue and generalised aches

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Constitutional symptoms are common in GCA and the PMR–GCA spectrum.
Malaise and aching raise the possibility of PMR, which overlaps with GCA.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of jaw claudication (no jaw pain on chewing)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Jaw claudication is highly specific for GCA; its absence lowers probability but does not exclude the diagnosis.
Scalp tenderness supports cranial arteritis; its absence modestly lowers probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of visual disturbance (no blurring, diplopia or vision loss)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No current visual symptoms argue against established ischaemic optic neuropathy, but do not remove the ongoing risk while GCA is unexcluded.
Reassuring but weak — visual loss in GCA is often sudden and can be the first ischaemic event.

symptoms.symptom_name: Headache partially relieved by simple analgesia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Response to paracetamol is consistent with a benign headache but is non-specific and does not exclude arteritis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute health anxiety triggered by a friend's phone call

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symptom amplification after an external alarming cue supports health anxiety as an exacerbating factor — but is a classic anchor that must not displace GCA exclusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Temporal arteries non-tender, non-thickened, with normal pulses on palpation

investigation_details.expected_result: Normal, non-tender, pulsatile temporal arteries bilaterally
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without palpation (impossible via telehealth), an important bedside sign of cranial arteritis cannot be assessed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal temporal arteries lower probability but do not exclude GCA; this is a sealed clinician finding unavailable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intact visual acuity, reactive pupils, full extraocular movements, normal fundal reflexes

investigation_details.expected_result: Visual acuity intact bilaterally, no RAPD, EOM full without diplopia, normal fundal reflexes
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The examination that excluded ischaemic optic neuropathy cannot be reproduced remotely, so the AI Doctor cannot confirm the eyes are safe.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal ophthalmic examination excludes current ischaemic optic neuropathy — but is a sealed clinician finding not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mildly elevated ESR (35 mm/hr) on recent bloods

loinc_ref.display: Erythrocyte sedimentation rate by Westergren method

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without same-day ESR/CRP the inflammatory activity underlying possible GCA cannot be gauged; a normal or mildly raised marker does not exclude GCA.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A mildly raised ESR is non-specific; markedly elevated ESR/CRP would raise suspicion, but normal markers do not exclude GCA.

symptoms.symptom_name: No proximal muscle weakness on examination (no PMR motor deficit)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: PMR causes stiffness/pain rather than true weakness; absence of weakness is expected and non-discriminating — a sealed clinician finding.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Older adult + new temporal headache + constitutional symptoms
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: This combination is the trigger to treat GCA as the important-not-to-miss diagnosis and to arrange urgent ESR/CRP and temporal artery/eye assessment, regardless of how anxious the presentation appears.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line inflammatory markers when GCA is suspected; must be obtained same day and interpreted alongside examination — but normal/mildly raised markers do not exclude GCA.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person temporal artery and ophthalmic examination
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The bedside assessment (temporal artery palpation, visual acuity, pupils, fundi) that the AI Doctor cannot perform and that is central to GCA exclusion.
Confirmatory investigation arranged by the assessing clinician/rheumatology if suspicion persists; not initiated by the AI Doctor.
Neuroimaging is not the first step for suspected GCA and should not be ordered to reassure; it does not diagnose cranial arteritis.
